tldr: Move from Didit's document/biometric KYC API to Pliance's Swedish AML platform when EU compliance, sanctions/PEP screening, and ongoing monitoring become the priority.
why_switch: Need ongoing AML monitoring (sanctions, PEP, adverse media) rather than one-off identity checks
EU/Nordic regulatory fit and GDPR-aligned hosting is becoming a hard requirement
Want CDD, screening, and transaction monitoring on one platform
Looking for developer-first AML APIs with detailed docs and SDKs
Reducing per-verification cost shape in favor of a custom EU-fintech contract
what_to_export: Verified user records and their KYC status
Document images and metadata (where retention policy permits)
PII fields you'll need on the Pliance side (name, DOB, country, IDs)
Mapping between Didit user IDs and your internal user IDs
export_method: Pull customer KYC data from Didit using the export mechanisms documented in their official API/docs, treating PII with the same care as production data (encrypted in transit and at rest). For artifacts you cannot legally retain post-cancellation, capture the decision metadata only. Coordinate with your DPO on what to actually move versus what to re-collect under a new lawful basis.
import_method: Stand up a Pliance tenant per their official documentation, configure your jurisdictions and screening lists, and load existing customers into their CDD/screening flows via the documented APIs. Re-screen migrated customers against sanctions/PEP/adverse-media to establish a fresh baseline, then enable ongoing monitoring. Wire webhooks for ongoing-monitoring alerts into your case-management system before going live.

concept_mapping.target: Pliance CDD inputs (often paired with a separate IDV vendor)
concept_mapping.note: Pliance focuses on AML; confirm whether you still need a doc/biometric vendor alongside
Operational shift from event-based to continuous
Re-implement webhook handlers against Pliance's event schema
Re-author rules; do not assume parity
Validate non-EU coverage before migrating those segments

gotchas: Pliance and Didit solve overlapping but different problems — you may need both an IDV and an AML vendor
PII migration must respect GDPR purpose limitation; involve legal/DPO early
Re-screening migrated users will surface new hits — staff case management before cutover
EU focus means non-EU coverage may be weaker; validate every region you serve
Custom pricing means TCO is opaque until you have a quote
Audit trail continuity matters for regulators — keep Didit records archived

email.subject: Migrating KYC/AML from Didit to Pliance
email.body:
Hi team,

Proposing we move our KYC/AML stack from Didit to Pliance, with a focus on EU compliance, sanctions/PEP screening, and ongoing monitoring.

Plan:
1. Loop in DPO/legal on what PII can be migrated under current lawful basis.
2. Export customer records, decisions, and audit logs from Didit per their docs.
3. Stand up a Pliance tenant per their official documentation; load CDD data and configure screening lists.
4. Re-screen migrated users to establish a baseline, then enable ongoing monitoring and wire alerts into case management.
5. Decide whether we still need an IDV vendor alongside Pliance for document/biometric checks.

Effort estimate 4-10 weeks. Pricing is custom on Pliance, so we'll need a quote.

Thanks
